rule_text: A trader should hedge directional risk in an option spread by executing an offsetting futures spread when the underlying futures contracts are imperfectly correlated.
entry_conditions: Option spread (long June/short March Eurodollar calls or puts) is initiated
Futures spread basis is measured at entry
Hedge ratio = 1.0x notional exposure of option spread
exit_conditions: Option spread is closed
Futures spread is closed simultaneously with options

The text is incomplete - cuts off mid-sentence describing the futures spread execution
haiku_reason: The signal has specific, actionable entry conditions (option spread initiation, futures spread basis measurement, and a concrete hedge ratio of 1.0x) that a programmer could detect from market data and portfolio state.

226 Chapter 11
Since conversions and reversals are common strategies in all option markets, a
trader who has an at-the-money conversion and is worried about pin risk can be fairly
certain that there a
rule_text: A trader holding a conversion or reversal position exposed to pin risk should seek to cross their position with an opposing trader at fair value to mutually eliminate pin risk.
entry_conditions: Position type is conversion or reversal
Underlying is at-the-money (strike within 0.5% of spot)
Days to expiration <= 14
Opposing position signal detected in market
exit_conditions: Cross executed at fair value with opposing position
Both positions eliminated simultaneously
hallucination_issues: Specific numeric thresholds (0.5% ATM distance, 14 days DTE) are not mentioned in source text
Source only mentions 'at-the-money' without defining precise proximity metrics
Pin risk timing context not explicitly stated as expiration-dependent in excerpt
haiku_reason: All entry conditions are specific and programmatically detectable: position type can be identified from portfolio data, ATM status uses a precise 0.5% threshold, DTE is a numerical comparison, and opposing signal detection can be implemented as a market data rule.

rule_text: As expiration approaches and model reliability decreases, reduce reliance on theoretical pricing models and shift toward experience-based decision-making.
entry_conditions: Days to expiration ≤ 5 days
Model reliability index < 0.85 (author: 'model is known to be incorrect')
exit_conditions: Switch to pure experience-based when DTE ≤ 2 days

haiku_reason: Both entry conditions are specific and quantifiable (DTE ≤ 5 days and Model reliability index < 0.85), allowing a programmer to code detection logic, even though the exit action ('experience-based decision-making') is subjective.

rule_text: When executing an option spread, hedge with a futures spread sized to achieve delta neutrality across the entire combined position.
entry_conditions: Option spread position is established
Calculate net delta: (long_option_delta × long_qty) - (short_option_delta × short_qty)
Net delta exposure is non-zero
exit_conditions: Close futures spread when option spread is closed
Rebalance if net delta drifts beyond ±25 deltas
hallucination_issues: Exit conditions contain hallucinated elements not mentioned in source text
Delta tolerance of 0 is too strict - source doesn't specify exact tolerance
Rebalancing trigger of ±25 deltas is completely fabricated
haiku_reason: The rule contains specific, actionable entry conditions (option spread establishment, delta calculation formula, non-zero delta threshold) and exit conditions (spread closure, delta drift beyond ±25) that a programmer could implement using portfolio state and Greeks data.

source_citation: Option Volatility and Pricing, Natenberg, Ch. 8, pp. 166–167
rule_text: A trader should avoid or actively hedge time spread positions when borrowing constraints exist or corporate actions (tender offers, stock calls) create forced assignment risk before the spread's intended expiration cycle completes.
entry_conditions: Long calendar/time spread established (long-dated call/put, short near-dated)
Stock borrow rate increases OR borrow availability drops below 100%
Corporate action announced (tender offer, special dividend, merger)
exit_conditions: Close entire spread at market upon any borrow constraint detection
Exit upon any M&A or corporate action announcement affecting underlying stock
hallucination_issues: Specific numerical thresholds (45 days, 100% borrow availability) not mentioned in source text
Borrow rate increases as trigger condition not explicitly stated in excerpt
Exit timing specificity ('immediately upon announcement') goes beyond source detail
Special dividends and mergers mentioned but not in source excerpt
haiku_reason: The signal has specific, detectable entry conditions (borrow rate increases, borrow availability drops below 100%, corporate action announcements) that can be monitored from market data feeds and portfolio systems, with clear exit triggers that a programmer can implement.

rule_text: When theoretical option value falls below intrinsic value (parity), adjust the valuation floor to parity rather than accepting below-intrinsic theoretical prices.
entry_conditions: Theoretical option value from Black-Scholes model calculated
Intrinsic value (parity) calculated for the option
Theoretical value < intrinsic value
exit_conditions: Use adjusted (parity) value for position sizing and risk management decisions

haiku_reason: All three entry conditions are specific and quantifiable: Black-Scholes theoretical value and intrinsic value can be calculated from market data, and their comparison is a concrete mathematical operation that code can execute.

Suppose a trader owns a stock which settles at a price of $50 when trading ceases
at 4:00 PM on the primary stock exchange. Suppose also that the trader has the right to
sell that stock to s
rule_text: A trader should exercise an embedded right (option) when market conditions move adversely relative to the exercise price, even if the original holding intention was passive, because the optionality provides asymmetric protection against unfavorable price movement.
entry_conditions: Underlying price at secondary exchange < (settlement_price × 0.98)
Embedded put/selling right is still available
Time to expiration > 1 hour remaining
exit_conditions: Exercise the right immediately upon threshold breach
Let option expire worthless if threshold not breached at expiration
hallucination_issues: The 2% threshold (0.98 multiplier) is inferred from the example ($50 to $48 = 4% drop) but not explicitly stated as a rule
The 1-hour minimum time to expiry is inferred but not specified in the text
Instrument categorized as 'OPTIONS_BUYING' when the example shows an embedded right/option that the trader already owns
Exit conditions are logical inferences but not explicitly stated in the excerpt
haiku_reason: The signal contains three specific, quantifiable entry conditions (price comparison to settlement threshold, embedded right availability, time remaining) that a programmer could detect from market data and portfolio state, combined with a clear action trigger (immediate exercise upon threshold breach).

rule_text: A trader should reduce or hedge short deeply in-the-money index option positions before market open to avoid unhedged delta exposure from overnight assignment that cannot be managed until the next trading day.
entry_conditions: Short index option position exists
Option moneyness > 1.5% in-the-money (intrinsic value / strike > 0.015)
Time to expiration ≤ 7 days
Approaching market close on expiration week
exit_conditions: Position closed before market close
Full delta hedge established (long underlying or long calls to offset)
All in-the-money short options removed from book
hallucination_issues: Specific moneyness threshold of 1.5% (0.015) not mentioned in source text
7-day expiration limit not specified in original excerpt
'Expiration week' timing not explicitly stated in source
Specific hedge ratios and implementation details not provided in source
haiku_reason: All entry conditions are specific and measurable (portfolio position existence, quantifiable moneyness threshold >1.5%, days to expiration ≤7, and identifiable time period), allowing programmatic detection from market data and portfolio state.

rule_text: A trader should reduce gamma exposure or hedge delta dynamically when holding short gamma positions with limited time to expiration, as high gamma amplifies losses from adverse price moves and gaps eliminate adjustment opportunities.
entry_conditions: Position contains short gamma (short straddle, strangle, or short options)
Gamma >= 15.0 per $1 move in underlying
Days to expiration <= 30
exit_conditions: Reduce or close entire position upon meeting entry conditions
Or hedge with long futures equal to delta before market opens
hallucination_issues: Gamma threshold of 15.0 is not mentioned in source text (actual examples show 2.2 and 38.1)
DTE threshold of 30 days is not specified in source (source only contrasts 9 months vs 1 day)
100% position reduction is not explicitly stated as the solution
Target regimes are not discussed in the excerpt
haiku_reason: The signal has specific, quantifiable entry conditions (short gamma position type, gamma >= 15.0, days to expiration <= 30) that a programmer could detect from portfolio data and market feeds, with clear exit actions (reduce position or hedge delta).

rule_text: A trader should reduce or avoid short positions in at-the-money options as expiration approaches, because traditional pricing models undervalue them and gaps in the underlying can cause catastrophic losses.
entry_conditions: Position is short options
Delta between -0.55 and +0.55 (ATM region)
Days to expiration ≤ 21 days
exit_conditions: Close or reduce all ATM short positions immediately if days-to-expiration ≤ 21

haiku_reason: All three entry conditions are specific and measurable (short position status, delta range 0.55, days to expiration ≤21), allowing programmatic detection from market data and portfolio state.

rule_text: A trader should hedge an options position by selling the underlying futures in proportion to the option's delta to establish a delta-neutral position.
entry_conditions: Long option position established with known delta
Underlying futures available for sale
Calculate hedge ratio: option_quantity × delta
exit_conditions: Close long option position
Delta moves beyond ±5% of neutral
Underlying contract expiration

haiku_reason: The signal has specific, actionable entry conditions (long option with known delta, futures available, explicit hedge ratio calculation) and exit conditions (position closure, delta threshold breach, contract expiration) that a programmer could detect from market data and portfolio state.

rule_text: A trader should close a hedge early when implied volatility has risen sufficiently to lock in a profit on the option leg, rather than holding until expiration.
entry_conditions: Hedge position is established (long/short futures paired with short/long options)
Implied volatility has increased from entry level
Option leg has gained 15% or more of its initial premium value
exit_conditions: Close entire hedge (exit both option and futures legs simultaneously)
Lock in profit from IV expansion on option leg

Instrument type 'SPREAD' is inferred - source describes futures-options hedge

haiku_reason: The signal has specific, measurable entry conditions (hedge position exists, IV increased, option leg gained 15%+ premium) that a programmer could detect from market data and portfolio state, and a clear exit action (close both legs simultaneously).

rule_text: A trader should construct volatility spreads with delta-neutral positioning, where directional exposure remains secondary to volatility exposure; if delta becomes large enough that directional considerations dominate, the position ceases to be a volatility spread and should be reconsidered or exited.
entry_conditions: Net position delta between -0.05 and +0.05
Position is constructed as a recognized volatility spread (backspread, long straddle, long strangle, short butterfly, etc.)
Gamma is positive for movement-beneficial spreads
exit_conditions: Absolute net delta exceeds 0.10
Directional PnL component becomes larger than volatility PnL component
Position no longer qualifies as delta-neutral volatility spread
hallucination_issues: Specific numerical delta thresholds (0.05, 0.10) are not mentioned in the source text
Parameters like 'rebalance_threshold' and 'delta_neutral_band' are not referenced in the excerpt
Target regimes 'RANGING' and 'HIGH_VOL' are inferred but not explicitly stated
The concept of comparing directional vs volatility PnL components is inferred from the text but not explicitly described
haiku_reason: The signal contains specific, quantifiable entry conditions (delta range -0.05 to +0.05, positive gamma) and exit conditions (delta exceeds 0.10) that a programmer could implement by monitoring portfolio Greeks and position characteristics against market data.

rule_text: A trader should rebalance their delta-neutral position based on a fixed delta threshold that matches their position size and risk capital, rather than on fixed time intervals.
entry_conditions: Position delta accumulates to ±100 deltas from neutral
Cumulative delta drift detected since last rebalance
exit_conditions: Execute rebalancing trades to return position to delta neutral

Target regimes not discussed in excerpt
haiku_reason: The entry conditions specify quantifiable triggers (position delta accumulates to ±100 deltas, cumulative delta drift detection) that can be programmatically monitored from portfolio state data, and the exit condition provides a clear action (rebalance to delta neutral).

rule_text: A trader should monitor time decay of positive gamma positions and exit before theta erosion eliminates the theoretical edge.
entry_conditions: Position has positive gamma characteristics
Position structure is long vega (spread 5 type)
Trade is initiated with known theta decay timeline
exit_conditions: Exit when calendar days elapsed exceed 50% of theta decay horizon (9 days)
Exit immediately if underlying shows no movement by day 9

Calculate required spread delta = desired_hedge_ratio × position_delta

Underlying moves to predefined boundary of protection range
Time decay reaches 50% of spread life

rule_text: When unable to liquidate a problematic options position, neutralize directional delta exposure by purchasing equivalent delta amount through alternative instruments (stock or futures) rather than closing the original position.
entry_conditions: Short options position has accumulated directional delta exposure
Liquidating the short options position is not preferred
Alternative instruments (futures or stock) are available for purchase
exit_conditions: Delta exposure is fully neutralized
Original short options position is eventually closed

haiku_reason: All three entry conditions are actionable: delta exposure can be calculated from portfolio positions, liquidation preference can be set as a trader rule/parameter, and alternative instrument availability can be determined from market data.
